assertions.text: V1 was entering the roadway from a parking spot.
V1 was attempting to make a U-turn to head west.
The roadway is a five-lane, two-way roadway.
V2 was traveling east in lane two of the five-lane roadway.
As V1 made the U-turn, the left side of V1 made contact with V2's front plane.
As the vehicles continued in motion, the left side of V1 struck the front of V2.
The contact between V1's left side and V2's front occurred while V1 was making the U-turn.
Vehicles continued in motion after the initial contact and a subsequent contact occurred (left side of V1 to front of V2).
There were at least two distinct contacts between V1 and V2 (initial contact of V1 left side with V2 front plane, then left side of V1 struck V2 front).
V1 came to final rest in westbound lanes of travel.
V2 came to final rest in the middle of the roadway.
No specific speeds for V1 or V2 were stated.

verdicts.evidence: Whether V1 was entering from a parking spot cannot be determined from V2 telemetry; sensors show V2 speed ranging from [5.0 km/h](T2) to [35.0 km/h](T2) but contain no data about V1 or parking location.
V2 telemetry does not contain V1 maneuver intent; steering shows a peak signed deviation of [30.0 deg](T2) on V2 but that does not indicate V1 attempting a U-turn.
Roadway lane count and configuration are not recorded by V2 sensors; vehicle info lists speed limit [40.0 km/h](T1) but no lane count.

V2 speed and steering samples show V2 was moving with speed values between [5.0 km/h](T2) and [35.0 km/h](T2) and had a steering peak of [30.0 deg](T2), but telemetry does not encode lane number or compass heading (east) so lane two and eastbound travel are unverifiable.

IMU and delta-V show a primary longitudinal delta-V of [−18.0 km/h](T2) and lateral delta-V peak [−5.0 km/h](T2), indicating a frontal with some lateral component, but device-frame axes and which object side contacted V2 cannot be determined from V2 data alone.

The telemetry records a crash with cumulative longitudinal delta-V [−18.0 km/h](T2) and lateral delta-V final [−2.0 km/h](T2), consistent with front contact, but cannot confirm that V1's left side specifically struck V2's front.

Timing shows steering activity peaking at [−0.5 s](T2) and brake applied at [−0.5 s](T2) before impact, and the crash pulse peaks at [0.1 s](T1), but whether V1 was mid U-turn at that moment is not available from V2 sensors.

There is a single crash pulse recorded, with cumulative longitudinal delta-V reaching [−18.0 km/h at t=0.1 s](T3) indicating an impact; pre-impact steering and brake transitions at [−0.5 s](T2,T3) show movement before impact — telemetry shows the vehicle was in motion up to impact with speed rising from [5.0 km/h at t=−5.0 s](T2) to [35.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2), supporting that motion continued through contact.

The sensors record a single crash event with CISS delta-V peaks of [−18.0 km/h longitudinal](T3) and [−5.0 km/h lateral](T3) and EDR summary lists [num_events_recorded: 1](T1), contradicting the claim of at least two distinct contacts between the vehicles.
Final rest positions (V1 rest in westbound lanes) are not recorded by V2 telemetry; V2 data contains no GPS position info to verify V1 final rest location.

V2 telemetry does not include absolute position to confirm that V2 came to rest in the middle of the roadway; speed last recorded at [35.0 km/h at t=0.0 s](T2) and crash pulse final delta-V [−18.0 km/h](T2) indicate impact but not final rest location.

The narrative gives no specific speeds; V2 data shows pre-crash speed samples with first [5.0 km/h](T2) and last [35.0 km/h](T2), and the EDR summary lists vehicle speed limit [40.0 km/h](T1), consistent with no explicit speeds stated for V1 or V2.

result.rationale: V2 telemetry clearly records a single crash pulse with cumulative delta-V [−18.0 km/h](T3) longitudinal and [−5.0 km/h](T3) lateral, and pre-impact speed samples from [5.0 km/h to 35.0 km/h](T2). However, most assertions concern V1's maneuvers, lane counts, and final positions which are not present in V2 sensor data, making them unverifiable.

uncited_quantitative_claims.context: g an impact; pre-impact steering and brake transitions at [−0.5 s](T2,T3) show movement before impact — telemetry shows the v

result.time_zero: impact (t=0); pre-crash negative, crash pulse positive

channels.name: delta_v_long
channels.desc: Cumulative longitudinal delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

channels.name: delta_v_lat
channels.desc: Cumulative lateral delta-V during impact (times converted from ms to s)

result.notes: NHTSA CISS EDR data: sparse pre-crash series (~2 Hz, t<0) plus a crash-pulse delta-V curve (t>=0, ~10 ms steps). t=0 is the impact.

delta_v_long.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

delta_v_lat.note: cumulative velocity change during the crash pulse

detections.criterion: crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum
crash-pulse cumulative delta-V at its maximum

result.thresholds_doc: impact spike: |a-baseline|>2.5g (>=20ms, merged<0.25s); sustained: 0.5s-avg in 0.35-1.2g; rotation: >40.0deg/s for 0.3s; net rotation >60.0deg; speed drop: >=15.0km/h in <=3.0s; brake: 0->1 transitions; CISS impact: max |crash-pulse delta-V|
